prs.title: [ROCm][Feature] Enable AITER MLA attention backend to work with Eagle3 speculative decoding on ROCm
prs.author: larryli2-amd
prs.body_head:
## Summary                                                                                      
                                                                                                  
  Enable AITER MLA attention backend to work with Eagle3 speculative decoding on ROCm.            
                                                                                                  
  AITER MLA is the fastest MLA attention backend on AMD MI300X/MI355X GPUs (using AMD's ASM       
  kernels via the aiter library). However, it currently cannot be used with speculative decoding
  methods like Eagle3 due to a `kernel_block_size` conflict:                                      
                                                                                                
  - **AITER MLA** declares `get_supported_kernel_block_sizes() = [1]`                             
  - **Eagle3 draft model** (flash_attn) requires `block_size = MultipleOf(16)`                    
  - Both share the same KV cache group, so `select_common_block_size()` fails — no block size      satisfies both constraints                                                                      
                                                 
  This means users must choose between AITER MLA (fast decode, no spec-dec) or a slower backend   
  (spec-dec works but lower baseline performance). This PR removes that limitation.               
                                                                                                  
  ## Key Insight                                                                                  
                                                 
  The aiter MLA decode kernel always operates with `page_size=1` internally — the vLLM wrapper    
  flattens `kv_buffer` via `.view(-1, 1, 1, head_size)` before calling the ASM kernel. The kernel
  never sees multi-token pages. So AITER MLA can work with any `kernel_block_size` — we just need 
  to exp

prs.title: [ROCm] Implement GPU-to-NUMA-node detection
prs.author: pschlan-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Implement automatic NUMA topology detection on ROCm platforms via the amdsmi library (which is already a dependency).

## Test Plan
Run vLLM with `--numa-bind`, observe correct topology detection

## Test Result
Topology is detected correctly on the test system with 8 GPUs connected to 2 NUMA nodes:
```INFO 04-16 12:57:41 [numa_utils.py:111] Auto-detected NUMA nodes for GPUs: [0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1]```

prs.title: [ROCm] Cast score correction bias tensor during model construction for DeepSeek/Kimi-K2
prs.author: heachary
prs.body_head: ## Purpose The moe score correction bias tensor was being cast to the gate output dtype on every forward pass. The datatype that this tensor needs to be cast to is known at model construction time and never changes beyond that. So this repeated cast is redundant work that launches an extra GPU kernel per MoE layer per forward call. This PR moves the cast to the model construction thereby eliminating the per-forward-pass overhead. ## Summary ### Before ### After The trace shows the elementwise operation kernel responsible for this typecast operation happening before the grouped-topk operation in every forward pass. With the following changes this typecast is shifted to model construction thereby avoiding the call during every forward pass: - **`vllm/model_executor/models/deepseek_v2.py`**: Pre-cast `e_score_correction_bias` to match `gate.out_dtype` during `DeepseekV2MoE` construction. Since all downstream consumers (FusedMoE, router) share the same `nn.Parameter` object, this single mutation propagates everywhere. - **`vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py`**: Replace the runtime `.to()` cast with an assert that the bias dtype already matches the gating output dtype, catching any future regression where the init-time cast is missed. - **`vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/router/fused_topk_bias_router.py`**: Same change — replace the runtime `.to()` cast with a matching assert. ## Test Result ### Accuracy Benchmark | Metric | Score | Threshold | Status -- | -- | -- | -- | -- GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (flexible-extract) | 0.936 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (strict-match) | 0.936 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS ### Performance |

prs.title: [WIP] Add TRITON_MLA_SPARSE backend for SM80 sparse MLA support
prs.author: haosdent
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Closes #38006. Enables sparse MLA models (GLM-5, DeepSeek-V3.2) on SM80 (A100/A800) and SM121 (GB10/DGX Spark), where DeepGEMM / FlashMLA-Sparse / FlashInfer-MLA-Sparse are unavailable.

Three changes:

1. **Guard fix.** `has_deep_gemm()` (package check) → `is_deep_gemm_supported()` (SM90+ arch check) in `sparse_attn_indexer.py` and `indexer.py`. Prevents DeepGEMM SM90+ kernels from being invoked on SM80/SM121 when the Python package happens to be installed.

2. **Triton indexer MQA logits.** `mqa_logits_triton.py` reproduces DeepGEMM's `fp8_mqa_logits` / `fp8_paged_mqa_logits` (bf16 tensor-core matmul with fp32 accumulator + scale, ReLU + per-head weighting, sum across heads). PyTorch reference stays in `mqa_logits_fallback.py`. On GB10, `fp8_paged_mqa_logits` at ctx=2048 goes from 6.4 ms (torch loop) → 0.03 ms (~250×), saving ~380 ms/token across 60 sparse layers.

3. **New `TRITON_MLA_SPARSE` backend** (`triton_mla_sparse.py` → `triton_sparse_mla_kernel.py`). Single pure-Triton kernel with split-KV for low-batch decode (`num_kv_splits > 1` when the single-pass grid under-fills the device) and a single-pass fast path otherwise. Stage 2 merges per-split `(out, lse)` tiles via online-softmax rescaling (pattern from `triton_decode_attention.py`). `@triton.autotune` over `(num_warps, num_stages)` × `(index_topk, NUM_KV_SPLITS)`; warmed in `TritonMLASparseImpl.__init__` so the first user request does not pay the sweep cost. Registered in `AttentionBackendEnum` and added to the non-SM100 MLA priority list.

**Kernel benchmark on NVIDIA A30 (SM80, 56 SMs):**

| shape | XPU kernel | this PR | speedup |
|---|---|---|---|
| nt=1, topk=2048, H=128 (decode) | 0.297 ms | 0.094 ms | **3.15×** |
| nt=1, topk=4096, H=128 | 0.580 ms | 0.167 ms | 3.46× |
| nt=1, topk=4096, H=16 | 0.582 ms | 0.081 ms | 7.17× |
| nt=128, topk=2048, H=128 (prefill) | 4.912 ms | 4.872 ms | 1.01× |

Decode (nt=1) across all (topk, H): median **4.30×**, best 7.17×. Non-decode (nt ≥ 8): autotu

prs.title: [ROCm][ViT] Detect Triton-AMD kernels at their new aiter location
prs.author: Lafunamor
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

`flash_attn_triton_available()` in `vllm/platforms/rocm.py` gates whether `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN` is offered for the ViT encoder on ROCm. The check looks for `flash_attn.flash_attn_triton_amd`, but ROCm/flash-attention commit [`3f94643`](https://github.com/ROCm/flash-attention/commit/3f94643) (2026-03-12, *\"[AMD] Migrate to Triton Backend to Aiter\"*) removed that subpackage and moved the kernels into aiter at `aiter.ops.triton._triton_kernels.flash_attn_triton_amd`.

After that migration, `find_spec(\"flash_attn.flash_attn_triton_amd\")` returns `None` on every current ROCm wheel, so the ViT silently falls back to `TORCH_SDPA`. On gfx1151 (Strix Halo / RDNA3.5) the SDPA path produces NaN/Inf image embeddings and the LM collapses into an endless `!` stream. Other ROCm targets just lose the fast path.

This PR widens the detection to accept either the legacy subpackage or the new aiter location, restoring Triton-AMD as an available ViT backend wherever the kernels actually live.

## Changes

`vllm/platforms/rocm.py` — `flash_attn_triton_available()`: return `False` only when **both** import paths are missing.

No behavior change when `flash_attn.flash_attn_triton_amd` still exists (older builds). When only the aiter path exists (current builds), the function now correctly returns `True`.

## Test Plan / Result

- Static: verified the new check returns `True` against an aiter build where only the new path exists, and `False` when neither path exists.
- On gfx1151, using the explicit `--mm-encoder-attn-backend TRITON_ATTN` flag (a different code path — vLLM's own `vit_triton_attn_wrapper`) I confirmed the Triton ViT kernels produce correct image embeddings on Qwen3-VL-A3B AWQ.

**Shortcoming I want to be transparent about:** I have *not* been able to end-to-end verify the auto-selected `AttentionBackendEnum.FLASH_ATTN` ViT path that this PR enables. In the environment I tested, aiter's JIT-built `.so`s are not discoverable by `importlib` due to a

prs.title: [Bugfix] Fix scaled_mm output narrowing for 3D input tensors
prs.author: nemanjaudovic
prs.body_head:
When the input activation x is 3D (e.g. [batch, seq, hidden_in]), the base class flattens it to 2D before calling apply_scaled_mm, but passes the original 3D shape as
  output_shape. The narrowing step then used output_shape[0] (batch only) instead of the full number of tokens (which for 3D inputs is output_shape[0] * output_shape[1], or in 
  general math.prod(output_shape[:-1])). Fix by using math.prod(output_shape[:-1]) in all three pytorch kernel variants so the correct number of rows is used regardless of    
  input rank.                                                                                                                                                                   
                  
  Co-authored-by: Claude
                                                                                                                                                                                
  Purpose                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                
  Fix incorrect output narrowing in pytorch FP8 scaled_mm kernels when input activations are 3D tensors. When initializing the Phi model with:                                  
```python                  
  from vllm import LLM                                                                                                                                                          
  llm = LLM(                                                                                                                                                                    
      model='nm-testing/Phi-3.5-vision-instruct-FP8-dynamic',

prs.title: [KV Offload] Pass request context
prs.author: omerpaz95
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Pass per-request context (`kv_transfer_params`) through the CPU offloading manager interface.

The `OffloadingManager` ABC and `CPUOffloadingManager` now accept a `ReqContext` argument on `lookup`, `prepare_load`, and `prepare_store`. `ReqContext` wraps `request.kv_transfer_params` (a free-form dict set via `sampling_params.extra_args`) and is constructed at each call site in `OffloadingConnectorScheduler`.

This is wiring-only: `ReqContext` is accepted but not yet consumed by `CPUOffloadingManager`.

Structural changes:
- `ReqContext` dataclass is defined in `vllm/v1/kv_offload/abstract.py` (shared layer) rather than `cpu/manager.py`, so other `OffloadingManager` implementations can use it without importing from the CPU-specific module.

## Test Plan

```bash
# Lint
cd vllm
pre-commit run

# Scheduler unit tests
pytest tests/v1/kv_connector/test_offloading_scheduler.py -v -s
```

## Test Result

Pre-commit (ruff, mypy, all hooks): all passed.

---
